explanation: EV (Expected Value) tells you whether a bet is mathematically profitable in the long run. It compares what YOU think the true probability is (model) against what the BOOKMAKER is paying you.

label: Formula
explanation: If EV is positive you are being overpaid relative to the true probability. If negative you are being underpaid.

label: Example — coin flip
explanation: Imagine a fair coin. True probability of heads = 50% (1/2). If bookie pays 2.20 for heads: EV = (0.50 x 2.20) - 1 = +10%. You'd bet this every time.

label: Where model_probability comes from
explanation: The model prices each team as a fair decimal odd (no margin). We invert that to get a probability. e.g. fair odds 2.00 = 50% probability.

label: Why 20% threshold?
explanation: In Year 1 we use a conservative 20% EV minimum before flagging a signal. This filters out noise and marginal edges. It means the model must think the bookie is mispricing by at least 20 cents in the dollar before we act.

label: Quarter Kelly on top
explanation: Even when EV clears 20% we size at quarter Kelly with a hard stake cap. High EV does not mean bet big — it means bet smart.
--- ROUND 7 RESULTS: ALL 8 GAMES RANKED BY EV ---

label: Game 1 — STRONGEST SIGNAL
explanation: THE MODEL DISAGREED WITH THE MARKET ON WHO WOULD WIN.

label: Model fair odds — Eels
explanation: Model thought Eels had a 30.6% chance of winning at home. This is low — model actually expected Bulldogs to win.

label: Model fair odds — Bulldogs
explanation: Model had Bulldogs as favourites. But not as strong a favourite as the market.

label: Bookie closing odds — Eels
explanation: Bookie priced Eels at 4.20 implying only 23.8% probability. Much lower than model's 30.6%.

label: Bookie closing odds — Bulldogs
explanation: Bookie had Bulldogs at 1.23 implying 81.3% probability. Model only gave them 69.4%.

label: EV on Eels (HOME)
explanation: The bookie was paying 4.20 for something the model thought was worth 3.27. That gap is the edge.

label: EV on Bulldogs (AWAY)
explanation: Bulldogs were overpriced by the market relative to model. No edge backing them.

label: Signal fired?
explanation: Only signal of the round that cleared the 20% threshold.

label: Result
explanation: Signal correct. Even though the MODEL expected Bulldogs to win outright — the EV logic correctly identified Eels as the value bet.

label: Key lesson
explanation: EV betting is not about picking winners. It is about finding where the market has mispriced probability. The model said Bulldogs would probably win but the market was overconfident — so the value was on Eels.

label: Model fair odds — Raiders
explanation: Model gave Raiders a slight home edge.

label: Bookie closing odds — Raiders
explanation: Market priced Raiders at 2.10 — much longer than model. Market thought Storm were more likely to win.

label: EV on Raiders
explanation: Just under 20% threshold. In strict rules this would not fire. But it is close — a borderline signal.

label: Result
explanation: Would have been a winner. Illustrates the cost of the 20% filter — you miss some edges. But over the long run the filter prevents acting on noise.

label: Game 3
value: New Zealand Warriors vs Gold Coast Titans

label: EV on Warriors
explanation: Model had Warriors as massive favourites (89.4%). Bookie had them at 1.29 — still shorter than model implied.

label: Result
explanation: Correct direction. Below 20% threshold so no signal fires.

label: Model fair odds — Dolphins
explanation: Model had Panthers winning. But not by as much as the market.

label: Bookie closing odds — Dolphins
explanation: Bookie priced Dolphins longer than model — market more confident in Panthers.

label: EV on Dolphins (HOME)
explanation: Edge on Dolphins despite model expecting Panthers to win. Same logic as Eels — value can sit on the underdog side.

label: Result
explanation: Wrong by one golden point kick. Close. Below 20% threshold so no signal.

label: Game 5
value: North Queensland Cowboys vs Manly Sea Eagles

label: EV on Cowboys (HOME)
explanation: Positive but below threshold.

label: Result
explanation: Biggest miss of round. Cowboys blown out. Correct that this did not pass the 20% filter — the model was not confident enough to generate a signal and the result validated that caution.

label: EV on Broncos (AWAY)
explanation: Model had Tigers as slight home favourites but bookie had Tigers shorter. Value on Broncos.

label: Result
explanation: Correct. Below threshold — no signal fired but would have been a winner.

label: EV on Roosters (HOME)
explanation: Very slim edge. Model and market close on this one.

label: Result
explanation: Correct direction. Effectively no edge — 3.5% EV is noise.

label: Game 8 — NO EDGE
value: South Sydney Rabbitohs vs St George Dragons

label: EV on Rabbitohs (HOME)
explanation: Negative EV. Market had Rabbitohs shorter than model.

label: EV on Dragons (AWAY)
explanation: Also negative. No edge on either side.

label: Signal fired?
explanation: This game is excluded from positive EV count. The market was efficient here.

label: Result
explanation: Irrelevant — there was no signal to evaluate.
--- SCORECARD ---

label: Games with positive EV (either side)
explanation: Rabbitohs/Dragons excluded — both sides negative

label: Games clearing 20% threshold
explanation: Eels only

label: Result on all positive EV signals (7 games)

explanation: --- WHAT THIS TELLS US ---

label: Insight 1
explanation: The one game that cleared 20% EV won. The two biggest losses (Cowboys blowout) did not clear the threshold — the model was uncertain enough that the filter correctly kept them out.

label: Insight 2
value: EV and picking winners are different things
explanation: The Eels signal is the clearest example. Model expected Bulldogs to win. EV logic said bet Eels. Eels won. The edge came from reading the market's overconfidence — not from predicting the outright winner.

label: Insight 3
value: 71% on positive EV is a strong sample result
explanation: One round is not statistically meaningful. But 71% on the right side of positive EV games is the pattern we want to see persist. Track this across every round.

label: Insight 4
value: Totals model showing consistent UNDER bias vs market
explanation: Model priced 7 of 8 games below the bookmaker total line and won 5 of those UNDER signals. This needs more rounds to confirm but it is the most interesting structural finding so far.

label: Insight 5
value: Handicap is the hardest market to beat
explanation: 4/8 on handicap cover. The closing line is the most efficient price in the market. Do not prioritise handicap signals over H2H or totals at this stage.
